rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings to existing features or declared variables
Declare helper boolean variables for range checks using the pattern 'eq var_name : bool = expr;'
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model
Use component type scopes for port-based assumptions and guarantees
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in 'annex agree {** ... **};'
Boolean expressions in AGREE require explicit type declarations for helper variables
Implementation scope contracts may use assign but not guarantee statements
Range assumptions should use named constants or visible feature references
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables
Do not convert Boolean domain values to integer comparisons
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts without proper variable representation
